body_txt: Talking can be hard, especially if you're speaking from the heart. Of course, this tendency is often played up in fiction to the point where characters find themselves unable to even say a particularly emotionally charged word or phrase, often an apology or declaration of love. This may be because it goes against the character's self-image, or simply because they're shy in general. This trait in Anime is most commonly associated with the Tsundere (particularly Type A) and the Dandere . Supertrope to I "Uh" You, Too , Gibberish of Love , and Starstruck Speechless . Compare to Cannot Spit It Out and Gagging on Your Words .
